seoTitle: Fix Instagram Stories Not Sharing to Facebook Stories
metaDescription: Instagram Stories won't cross-post to Facebook even with sharing enabled? Fix the account connection and Meta Accounts Center settings causing it.
intro: When Instagram Stories stop sharing to Facebook Stories even though cross-posting is turned on, the cause is usually a broken account connection, an app glitch, or a setting that reset inside Meta Accounts Center. This guide walks through reconnecting your accounts and re-enabling story sharing so posts flow between both platforms again. It also covers what to check if the toggle keeps switching off on its own.

steps.heading: Check your Instagram–Facebook account connection
steps.body: Story sharing depends on your Instagram account being properly linked to Facebook, so confirm the connection is active before troubleshooting further.

steps.heading: Enable story sharing in Accounts Center
steps.body: Meta manages cross-posting permissions through Accounts Center, so make sure the story sharing toggle is switched on there, not just within the Instagram app.

steps.heading: Address disabled toggles or permission errors
steps.body: If sharing keeps turning off automatically, it's often linked to a permissions error or an outdated app version that needs to be resolved.

steps.heading: Confirm the option is visible and working
steps.body: After reconnecting and enabling sharing, test posting a new story to confirm it appears on Facebook as expected.

faq.question: Why did my Instagram Stories stop sharing to Facebook automatically?
faq.answer: This usually happens because of a broken account connection, an app glitch, or a setting that got disabled inside Meta Accounts Center.

faq.question: Where do I turn on story sharing between Instagram and Facebook?
faq.answer: Story sharing is controlled through Meta Accounts Center, which links your Instagram and Facebook accounts and manages cross-posting permissions.

faq.question: Why does the sharing toggle keep turning off by itself?
faq.answer: This can be caused by permission errors or an outdated app version, both of which are worth checking if the setting won't stay enabled.

faq.question: Do I need a linked Facebook account for this to work?
faq.answer: Yes, your Instagram account needs to be properly connected to a Facebook account for stories to cross-post at all.

faq.question: Is this issue related to Instagram or Facebook specifically?
faq.answer: It typically involves the connection between both platforms through Meta, rather than being isolated to just one app.
